_readme: ACM0014 v8.0 Policy C -- Monitoring Report test fixture. Field values are a realistic, internally-consistent dataset based on the Chok Chai Starch Co. wastewater treatment project (Thailand, CDM/ACM0014, DOE-verified). This exact structure produced a clean end-to-end mint (submit -> VVB verify -> mint) in live Guardian dry-run testing, twice, with no credentialSubject.undefined errors.
_section_2_baseline_methane_inputs: Bo, GWP, lagoon depth, reference-period COD. cin MUST be greater than cout (COD is removed by baseline treatment, not created).
_section_3_monthly_digester_flow_m3: Derived from real digester specs: 4650 m3 active volume / 1.87-day HRT = ~75,594 m3/month steady-state. Simplified here to a round 45000 for a smaller reference facility.
_section_4_monthly_cod_concentration_tCO: Typical raw starch-processing wastewater COD concentration.
_section_5_monthly_temperature_kelvin: Realistic tropical seasonal profile (~28 degC year-round).
_section_6_electricity_emissions_off: elflag=0: this test run has electricity baseline switched off. Set field56=1 and populate field57-60 to exercise this branch.
_section_7_heat_emissions_off: hgflag=0: heat baseline switched off in this test run. Set field61=1 and populate field62-64 to exercise this branch.
_section_8_land_application_active: laflag=1: this test run DOES exercise the land-application branch.
_section_9_solid_waste_diversion_off: smflag=0: not exercised in this test run.
_section_10_flaring_active: flrflag=1: this test run DOES exercise the flaring branch.
_section_11_direct_totals: Direct digester project emissions and leakage, entered per methodology guidance.

_expected_computed_outputs_confirmed_in_.field54_bey: ~33228 (varies with exact inputs -- this is BE_CH4 only)
_expected_computed_outputs_confirmed_in_.field55_ery_MINT_FIELD: ~32800 (this is the final tCO2e amount that gets minted)
_expected_computed_outputs_confirmed_in_.field67_bey_total: same as field54 since electricity/heat baselines are off in this run

_note_on_negative_test: To test the division-by-zero / negative-denominator guard specifically, set field6 (cin) LOWER than field7 (cout) -- e.g. field6=1200, field7=1800. Expected result: field52 (fTy) resolves cleanly to 0, and field54/field55 compute from there -- NOT null, NOT an unevaluated formula string. If you see null or a literal formula string in those fields, the guard has regressed.
